description_long: This is a weathered, cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top and three protruding hose connection outlets, two of which are capped with large, round metal covers and one with a smaller cap. The hydrant is primarily painted a faded yellow, with blue accents around the outlets and caps, and shows visible signs of rust and wear, especially near the base and around the outlets. The top of the hydrant features a central operating nut, which is slightly discolored from use and exposure. Embossed manufacturer or model markings are visible on the body, though partially obscured by grime and corrosion. The base is flanged with bolt holes, indicating it is designed to be bolted securely to the ground. No chains are attached to the caps, and the hydrant appears robust and heavy, typical of cast iron construction.
description: A weathered yellow fire hydrant with blue-accented hose outlets, rust patches, and embossed markings, featuring three capped connections and a domed top.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large capped outlets with blue rings and visible rust, as well as the central operating nut on top.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the embossed markings on the body and the smaller capped outlet facing forward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant with the third outlet and less visible rust, as well as the flanged base.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the large capped outlet and the blue accent, with rust and wear more prominent on the lower body.
